content:
Write a fuzzing harness for ONE specific attack surface of `hdf5` (c, address).

Attack surface: Committed datatype open and introspection
Target API(s): H5Topen2, H5Tget_class, H5Tget_size, H5Tget_nmembers, H5Tget_member_type, H5Tget_member_offset, H5Tis_variable_str, H5Tclose
Why this surface: Committed (named) datatypes are stored in the file and decoded on H5Topen2. Compound, enum, vlen, and array type descriptors are deeply nested and hand-parsed; historically a rich source of OOB/UAF bugs.

This is a follow-up attempt. Feedback on the previous harness:
Replace the golden-image overlay approach with one that feeds fuzzer bytes directly as raw HDF5 file content (similar to h5_read_fuzzer.c): copy fuzz data into an in-memory buffer, open it via H5Pset_file_image + CORE VFD, then call H5Topen2 for a set of candidate names. This eliminates the hard H5Fopen gate caused by heavy mutation. If the golden-image approach is kept, reduce SAFE_OFFSET from 512 to 8 (just the HDF5 magic "\x89HDF\r\n\x1a\n") so the fuzzer can mutate the superblock and object-header regions where committed type descriptors are actually encoded; the current 512-byte protection zone prevents the fuzzer from reaching the datatype-parsing code at all. Also avoid silently truncating inputs longer than the golden image — instead allocate max(golden_sz, size + SAFE_OFFSET) to allow the fuzzer to explore larger type descriptor payloads.

Address it: if it was a false-positive crash, fix the API misuse; if the surface was not explored
thoroughly enough, improve how the harness drives these APIs.

Follow CLAUDE.md for the full workflow (download, write into fuzz-proj, build green via build-project,
write /work/agent/new-harnesses.txt). Exercise the API the way a real caller would — a crash from
harness misuse is a false positive and will be rejected.

message.content:
Write a fuzzing harness for ONE specific attack surface of `hdf5` (c, address).

Attack surface: Committed datatype open and introspection
Target API(s): H5Topen2, H5Tget_class, H5Tget_size, H5Tget_nmembers, H5Tget_member_type, H5Tget_member_offset, H5Tis_variable_str, H5Tclose
Why this surface: Committed (named) datatypes are stored in the file and decoded on H5Topen2. Compound, enum, vlen, and array type descriptors are deeply nested and hand-parsed; historically a rich source of OOB/UAF bugs.

This is a follow-up attempt. Feedback on the previous harness:
Replace the golden-image overlay approach with one that feeds fuzzer bytes directly as raw HDF5 file content (similar to h5_read_fuzzer.c): copy fuzz data into an in-memory buffer, open it via H5Pset_file_image + CORE VFD, then call H5Topen2 for a set of candidate names. This eliminates the hard H5Fopen gate caused by heavy mutation. If the golden-image approach is kept, reduce SAFE_OFFSET from 512 to 8 (just the HDF5 magic "\x89HDF\r\n\x1a\n") so the fuzzer can mutate the superblock and object-header regions where committed type descriptors are actually encoded; the current 512-byte protection zone prevents the fuzzer from reaching the datatype-parsing code at all. Also avoid silently truncating inputs longer than the golden image — instead allocate max(golden_sz, size + SAFE_OFFSET) to allow the fuzzer to explore larger type descriptor payloads.

Address it: if it was a false-positive crash, fix the API misuse; if the surface was not explored
thoroughly enough, improve how the harness drives these APIs.

Follow CLAUDE.md for the full workflow (download, write into fuzz-proj, build green via build-project,
write /work/agent/new-harnesses.txt). Exercise the API the way a real caller would — a crash from
harness misuse is a false positive and will be rejected.

file.content:
#!/bin/bash -eu
# Copyright 2023 Google LLC.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
################################################################################

export LDFLAGS="${CFLAGS}"
export CMAKE_C_FLAGS="${CC} ${CFLAGS}"
export CMAKE_CXX_FLAGS="${CXX} ${CXXFLAGS}"

mkdir build-dir
cd build-dir
cmake -G "Unix Makefiles" \
    -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE:STRING=Release \
    -DBUILD_SHARED_LIBS:BOOL=OFF \
    -DBUILD_TESTING:BOOL=OFF \
    -DCMAKE_VERBOSE_MAKEFILES:BOOL=ON \
    -DHDF5_BUILD_EXAMPLES:BOOL=OFF \
    -DHDF5_BUILD_TOOLS:BOOL=OFF \
    -DHDF5_ENABLE_SANITIZERS:BOOL=ON \
    -DHDF5_ENABLE_Z_LIB_SUPPORT:BOOL=ON \
    ..

# Make the build verbose for easy logging inspection
cmake --build . --verbose --config Release -j$(nproc)
cd $SRC/hdf5

$CC $CFLAGS  -std=c99 -c \
  -I/src/hdf5/src -I/src/hdf5/build-dir/src -I./src/H5FDsubfiling/ \
  $SRC/h5_read_fuzzer.c
$CXX $CXXFLAGS $LIB_FUZZING_ENGINE h5_read_fuzzer.o ./build-dir/bin/libhdf5.a -lz -o $OUT/h5_read_fuzzer

$CC $CFLAGS  -std=c99 -c \
  -I/src/hdf5/src -I/src/hdf5/build-dir/src -I./src/H5FDsubfiling/ \
  $SRC/h5_extended_fuzzer.c
$CXX $CXXFLAGS $LIB_FUZZING_ENGINE h5_extended_fuzzer.o ./build-dir/bin/libhdf5.a -lz -o $OUT/h5_extended_fuzzer

zip -j $OUT/h5_extended_fuzzer_seed_corpus.zip $SRC/hdf5/test/testfiles/*.h5

# Build test
mkdir $SRC/hdf5/build-test
cd $SRC/hdf5/build-test
cmake -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE:STRING=Release -DBUILD_TESTING:BOOL=ON ..
cmake --build . --verbose --config Release -j$(nproc)

Now I have a good understanding of the approach. Let me create a harness that:

1. Uses the in-memory buffer approach (H5Pset_file_image + CORE VFD) similar to h5_read_fuzzer.c
2. Directly passes fuzz bytes as raw HDF5 file content
3. Tries to open committed datatypes with multiple candidate names
4. If a type is found, calls H5Tget_class, H5Tget_size, H5Tget_nmembers, H5Tget_member_type, H5Tget_member_offset, H5Tis_variable_str, H5Tclose
5. Now I'll write the fuzzing harness that implements this strategy.

Writing the harness...

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash -eu
# Copyright 2023 Google LLC.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
################################################################################

export LDFLAGS="${CFLAGS}"
export CMAKE_C_FLAGS="${CC} ${CFLAGS}"
export CMAKE_CXX_FLAGS="${CXX} ${CXXFLAGS}"

mkdir build-dir
cd build-dir
cmake -G "Unix Makefiles" \
    -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE:STRING=Release \
    -DBUILD_SHARED_LIBS:BOOL=OFF \
    -DBUILD_TESTING:BOOL=OFF \
    -DCMAKE_VERBOSE_MAKEFILES:BOOL=ON \
    -DHDF5_BUILD_EXAMPLES:BOOL=OFF \
    -DHDF5_BUILD_TOOLS:BOOL=OFF \
    -DHDF5_ENABLE_SANITIZERS:BOOL=ON \
    -DHDF5_ENABLE_Z_LIB_SUPPORT:BOOL=ON \
    ..

# Make the build verbose for easy logging inspection
cmake --build . --verbose --config Release -j$(nproc)
cd $SRC/hdf5

$CC $CFLAGS  -std=c99 -c \
  -I/src/hdf5/src -I/src/hdf5/build-dir/src -I./src/H5FDsubfiling/ \
  $SRC/h5_read_fuzzer.c
$CXX $CXXFLAGS $LIB_FUZZING_ENGINE h5_read_fuzzer.o ./build-dir/bin/libhdf5.a -lz -o $OUT/h5_read_fuzzer

$CC $CFLAGS  -std=c99 -c \
  -I/src/hdf5/src -I/src/hdf5/build-dir/src -I./src/H5FDsubfiling/ \
  $SRC/h5_extended_fuzzer.c
$CXX $CXXFLAGS $LIB_FUZZING_ENGINE h5_extended_fuzzer.o ./build-dir/bin/libhdf5.a -lz -o $OUT/h5_extended_fuzzer

zip -j $OUT/h5_extended_fuzzer_seed_corpus.zip $SRC/hdf5/test/testfiles/*.h5

# Build test
mkdir $SRC/hdf5/build-test
cd $SRC/hdf5/build-test
cmake -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE:STRING=Release -DBUILD_TESTING:BOOL=ON ..
cmake --build . --verbose --config Release -j$(nproc)

content.text:
Build is green. Here's a summary of what the harness does:

**Approach (in-memory CORE VFD):**
- Treats the entire fuzz input as raw HDF5 file bytes — no golden-image overlay, no protected region
- Uses `H5Pset_fapl_core` + `H5Pset_file_image` to load the fuzz buffer directly into the CORE VFD, then calls `H5Fopen("fuzz_image", H5F_ACC_RDONLY, fapl)` — if the bytes aren't a valid HDF5 file it returns an error and we exit cleanly
- This lets the fuzzer mutate the superblock, object-header, and all committed-type descriptor bytes

**API coverage:**
1. `H5Topen2` — tried against 17 candidate names *and* every name discovered by walking the root group
2. For each successfully opened type: `H5Tget_class`, `H5Tget_size`, `H5Tis_variable_str`
3. For compound types: `H5Tget_nmembers`, then per-member `H5Tget_member_offset` + `H5Tget_member_type` (recursive, up to depth 8)
4. For enum types: `H5Tget_nmembers` + `H5Tget_member_offset`
5. For array/vlen types: `H5Tget_super` + recursive introspection
6. `H5Tclose` on every handle
The background build task has completed successfully (exit code 0). The harness `h5_committed_type_fuzzer` is built and registered in `/work/agent/new-harnesses.txt`.
